agent_response.reasoning: This is a thermodynamics problem involving electrical heating of water. I need to: 1) Calculate the electrical energy input using P = I²R, 2) Convert this energy to heat added to the water, 3) Use the heat capacity of water to find the temperature change, 4) Add this to the initial temperature to get the final temperature.
assumptions: All electrical energy is converted to heat and transferred to the water (100% efficiency)
Water remains in liquid phase throughout heating
Heat capacity of liquid water is constant at 4.18 J/(g·K)
agent_response.method: Energy balance approach using Q = mcΔT. First calculate electrical energy input using Joule heating (P = I²R), then use this as heat input to calculate temperature rise.
agent_response.skill_notes: For electrical heating problems: (1) Use P = I²R to find power, (2) Multiply by time for total energy, (3) Apply Q = mcΔT for temperature change. Key is ensuring all electrical energy converts to thermal energy in the water. Always check that water doesn't reach boiling point at given pressure.
